---
title: Installer Configuration Reference
description: Reference for every file, credential, and environment variable generated by the Alexandria installer.
---

import { Aside, FileTree } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

After a successful installation, the Alexandria installer writes several configuration files to your install directory. This page documents every generated file, credential, port assignment, and Docker volume.

## Generated Files

A production install with all features enabled produces this layout:

<FileTree>

- alexandria/
  - docker-compose.yml — assembled Docker Compose stack
  - .env — credentials and environment variables
  - garage.prod.toml — Garage S3 daemon configuration
  - prometheus.yml — Prometheus scrape config (monitoring only)
  - docker-compose.override.yml — debug port bindings (local preview only)
  - alexandria-config.json — full install record with all secrets
  - backups/ — directory for PostgreSQL backup output

</FileTree>

### docker-compose.yml

The main compose file is assembled from per-service template fragments. Which services are included depends on your feature selections:

| Service | Always included | Condition |
|---------|:--------------:|-----------|
| postgres | Yes | — |
| garage | Yes | — |
| garage-init | Yes | — |
| rabbitmq | Yes | — |
| api | Yes | — |
| document-worker | Yes | — |
| frontend | Yes | — |
| postgres-backup | Yes | — |
| media-worker | No | Media processing enabled |
| prometheus | No | Monitoring enabled |
| grafana | No | Monitoring enabled |

All `{{TOKEN}}` placeholders are replaced with your generated credentials and port assignments at install time.

### .env

The environment file contains all credentials and port assignments used by Docker Compose:

```bash title=".env"
# --- Database ---
DB_PASSWORD=<generated>

# --- Garage S3 Storage ---
GARAGE_RPC_SECRET=<generated>
GARAGE_ADMIN_TOKEN=<generated>
GARAGE_METRICS_TOKEN=<generated>
GARAGE_S3_ACCESS_KEY=<generated>
GARAGE_S3_SECRET_KEY=<generated>

# --- RabbitMQ ---
RABBITMQ_USER=alexandria
RABBITMQ_PASSWORD=<generated>
RABBITMQ_VHOST=alexandria

# --- JWT Authentication ---
JWT_SECRET=<generated>
JWT_ISSUER=alexandria
JWT_AUDIENCE=alexandria-users

# --- CSRF Protection ---
CSRF_SECRET=<generated>

# --- CORS ---
CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=

# --- Frontend ---
FRONTEND_PORT=80
```

### garage.prod.toml

Configuration for the Garage S3-compatible object store:

```toml title="garage.prod.toml"
metadata_dir = "/var/lib/garage/meta"
data_dir = "/var/lib/garage/data"
db_engine = "lmdb"

replication_factor = 1
compression_level = 1

rpc_bind_addr = "[::]:3901"
rpc_public_addr = "garage:3901"
rpc_secret = "<generated>"

[s3_api]
s3_region = "garage"
api_bind_addr = "[::]:3900"
root_domain = ".s3.garage.localhost"

[s3_web]
bind_addr = "[::]:3902"
root_domain = ".web.garage.localhost"

[admin]
api_bind_addr = "[::]:3903"
admin_token = "<generated>"
metrics_token = "<generated>"
```

<Aside type="tip">
  Do not change `replication_factor` or `db_engine` after the initial deployment without performing a full data migration. These settings are baked into the storage layout.
</Aside>

### prometheus.yml

Written only when monitoring is enabled. Configures Prometheus to scrape the API health endpoint and Garage metrics:

```yaml title="prometheus.yml"
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s
  evaluation_interval: 15s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: "alexandria-api"
    metrics_path: /health
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["api:8080"]

  - job_name: "garage"
    metrics_path: /metrics
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["garage:3903"]
```

### docker-compose.override.yml

Written only for **local preview** mode. Docker Compose automatically merges this with `docker-compose.yml` to expose debug ports on the host:

```yaml title="docker-compose.override.yml"
services:
  postgres:
    ports:
      - "<POSTGRES_PORT>:5432"
  rabbitmq:
    ports:
      - "<RABBITMQ_MANAGEMENT_PORT>:15672"
```

This allows you to connect to PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ Management directly from your host machine during local testing.

### alexandria-config.json

A machine-readable JSON record of the entire installation. Contains the full configuration including all secrets in plaintext.

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `Environment` | enum | `Deployment` or `LocalPreview` |
| `S3Provider` | enum | Always `Garage` (current version) |
| `Features` | object | `MediaProcessing`, `Monitoring`, port numbers |
| `Ports` | dict | Final assigned ports keyed by env var name |
| `OriginalPorts` | dict | Pre-remap defaults (empty if no remapping occurred) |
| `Credentials` | dict | All generated secrets (full plaintext values) |
| `InstallPath` | string | Absolute path to the install directory |
| `ProjectSourcePath` | string | Path to the project source (may differ from install path in production) |
| `InstalledAt` | ISO 8601 | UTC timestamp of when the installer ran |

<Aside type="caution">
  `alexandria-config.json` contains all secrets in plaintext. If your install directory is inside a Git repository, add it to `.gitignore` immediately.
</Aside>

## Credential Reference

All credentials are generated using `System.Security.Cryptography.RandomNumberGenerator` — cryptographically secure.

| Variable | Purpose | Format |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `DB_PASSWORD` | PostgreSQL password for `alexandria_user` | 24-char alphanumeric + symbols |
| `GARAGE_RPC_SECRET` | Garage cluster RPC authentication | 64-char hex string |
| `GARAGE_ADMIN_TOKEN` | Garage Admin API bearer token | 64-char hex string |
| `GARAGE_METRICS_TOKEN` | Garage metrics endpoint token | 64-char hex string |
| `GARAGE_S3_ACCESS_KEY` | S3 access key (AWS-style) | 20-char uppercase alphanumeric |
| `GARAGE_S3_SECRET_KEY` | S3 secret key | 24-char alphanumeric + symbols |
| `RABBITMQ_USER` | RabbitMQ username | Static: `alexandria` |
| `RABBITMQ_PASSWORD` | RabbitMQ password | 24-char alphanumeric + symbols |
| `RABBITMQ_VHOST` | RabbitMQ virtual host | Static: `alexandria` |
| `JWT_SECRET` | JWT signing key | 64-char hex string |
| `JWT_ISSUER` | JWT `iss` claim value | Static: `alexandria` |
| `JWT_AUDIENCE` | JWT `aud` claim value | Static: `alexandria-users` |
| `CSRF_SECRET` | CSRF token signing key | 64-char hex string |
| `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` | Comma-separated allowed CORS origins | Empty by default |
| `GRAFANA_ADMIN_PASSWORD` | Grafana admin UI password | 24-char alphanumeric + symbols (monitoring only) |

<Aside type="tip">
  `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` is intentionally left empty. After deployment, edit `.env` and set it to your frontend's public URL (e.g., `https://alexandria.example.com`).
</Aside>

## Port Reference

| Variable | Service | Default (Production) | Default (Local Preview) | When exposed |
|----------|---------|---------------------|------------------------|-------------|
| `FRONTEND_PORT` | Nginx frontend | 80 | 3000 | Always |
| `GRAFANA_PORT` | Grafana | 3000 | 3001 | Monitoring only |
| `POSTGRES_PORT` | PostgreSQL | Not exposed | 5432 | Local preview only |
| `RABBITMQ_MANAGEMENT_PORT` | RabbitMQ Management UI | Not exposed | 15672 | Local preview only |

In production mode, PostgreSQL and RabbitMQ are only accessible within the Docker network. In local preview mode, the `docker-compose.override.yml` exposes them to the host for debugging.

## RAM Requirements

| Configuration | Minimum RAM |
|--------------|-------------|
| Base stack (no optional features) | 2560 MB |
| + Media processing | 3584 MB |
| + Monitoring only | 3072 MB |
| + Both features | 4096 MB |

The base stack includes: PostgreSQL (512 MB), Garage (512 MB), RabbitMQ (512 MB), API (512 MB), document-worker (512 MB), frontend (128 MB), plus headroom.

<Aside type="caution">
  In production mode, the installer hard-blocks if detected RAM is below 50% of the calculated minimum. In local preview mode, it only warns.
</Aside>

## Docker Volumes

All named volumes persist across `docker compose down`:

| Volume | Contents | Feature |
|--------|----------|---------|
| `postgres_data` | PostgreSQL database files | Always |
| `garage_meta_data` | Garage metadata (bucket list, key index) | Always |
| `garage_storage_data` | Garage stored objects (your uploaded files) | Always |
| `rabbitmq_data` | RabbitMQ queues and messages | Always |
| `prometheus_data` | Prometheus time-series metrics | Monitoring only |
| `grafana_data` | Grafana dashboards and settings | Monitoring only |

<Aside type="tip">
  `docker compose down` does **not** remove named volumes — your data is safe. Only use `docker compose down -v` if you want a complete data wipe.
</Aside>
